summary: Typosquat of `codebuff` fetching opaque binaries from a personal GitHub fork
details: Package `codebuff-cli` impersonates the legitimate CodebuffAI `codebuff` package by reproducing the official README verbatim (which instructs users to `npm install -g codebuff`), while the bin script in `cli/bin/codebuff.cjs` downloads its actual runtime from `Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli` — an unrelated personal GitHub account, not the CodebuffAI organization. On first invocation the script queries `https://api.github.com/repos/Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli/releases/latest` (a mutable pointer the repo owner can update at any time), downloads `codebuff-<platform>-<arch>` to `~/.codebuff/bin/`, runs `chmod 755`, and spawns the binary with the user's argv. There is no hash check, no signature verification, and no version pin — the fetched bytes are opaque executables under the personal account's full control. The combination of name impersonation, copied README directing users to a different package, and a mutable-tag binary dropper from a non-publisher account gives the personal-account owner ongoing arbitrary code execution on every installer's machine each time the CLI runs or refreshes its cached binary.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
